name: Conjure Work
description: Conjure, also known as rootwork, hoodoo, or tricking, is a system of African-American folk magic rooted in West and Central African spiritual traditions, combined with European folklore and Native American herbal knowledge. It developed in the context of slavery and oppression, serving as a means of survival, resistance, and empowerment. Conjure is a practical magic, focused on solving everyday problems such as love, money, health, and protection. It relies on the manipulation of natural objects, such as herbs, roots, minerals, and animal parts, as well as the power of prayer, incantations, and spiritual connection. Conjure is not a religion, but rather a set of practices that can be incorporated into other belief systems. It emphasizes personal agency and the ability to influence one's own destiny through spiritual means. The transmission of conjure knowledge is often oral, passed down through families and communities. Conjure recognizes the interconnectedness of all things and the importance of respecting the spiritual forces that govern the world. It is a dynamic and evolving tradition that continues to be practiced today.
subtitle: African-American folk magic and spirituality

keyMyths.title: The Origin of Conjure
keyMyths.description: Conjure originated as a blend of African spiritual practices and European folk magic during slavery. It provided enslaved Africans with a means of resistance and self-empowerment.

keyMyths.title: The Power of Roots
keyMyths.description: Roots are believed to possess potent spiritual energies that can be harnessed for healing, protection, and other purposes. Rootwork focuses on the skillful manipulation of these natural forces.

sources.author: Zora Neale Hurston
sources.description: A collection of African-American folklore, including stories of conjure and hoodoo.
sources.text: A collection of African-American folklore, including stories of conjure and hoodoo.

sources.author: Harry Middleton Hyatt
sources.description: A multi-volume collection of interviews with conjure practitioners in the Southern United States.
sources.text: A multi-volume collection of interviews with conjure practitioners in the Southern United States.
